title: reconnect_sse() recreates the channel to discard stale StreamEnded messages
description: reconnect_sse() aborts the old SSE task and creates a brand-new tokio unbounded_channel, replacing both sse_tx and sse_rx. The old sse_rx is dropped implicitly when the caller assigns the new one. This is load-bearing: the aborted task may have already sent a StreamEnded (or a stale StateChange) into the old channel before the abort took effect. If the channel were reused, that stale message would be received on the next recv() and trigger a second reconnect loop. By dropping the old receiver, all buffered messages from the dead task are discarded atomically. A future contributor might try to reuse the existing channel to avoid the allocation — that would be incorrect.
close_reason: Good decision already implemented in current codebase
